name: Agent MCP Catalog
description: MCP servers compatible with the Recomputable Agents kit. Every tool call routed through these is attested and recomputable.
howToAdd: Gateway admin -> /admin/mcps -> Add MCP server (name, endpoint URL, auth). Then assign per-agent. Calls flow through the attestation pipeline and are recomputable on /verify.

mcps.name: OpenSea
mcps.summary: Reads collection stats, floor prices and live listings, and prepares buy calldata so the agent can surface real NFTs you can actually purchase.
mcps.example: Show me some NFTs currently listed in the goblinarinos721 collection on OpenSea, with images and prices.

mcps.name: LI.FI
mcps.summary: Finds the best bridge-and-swap route across 30+ chains for ETH and ERC-20 tokens, returning a ready-to-sign quote with fees and timing.
mcps.example: Find the best route to bridge 0.1 ETH from Ethereum to Base and summarise it.

mcps.name: Symbiosis
mcps.summary: Returns read-only quotes and calldata to move value between chains in a single step.
mcps.example: Quote a cross-chain swap of 100 USDC from Ethereum to Arbitrum and summarise the route.

mcps.name: 1inch
mcps.summary: Finds the best swap price across dozens of liquidity sources, plus limit orders and portfolio data. Swaps ask for the user's wallet to sign.
mcps.example: Show the best price to swap 1 ETH into USDC on Ethereum (quote only).

mcps.name: Flashbots
mcps.summary: Private-mempool protection: sends transactions privately, simulates bundles and checks status, shielding trades from front-running and sandwich MEV.
mcps.example: Check the latest Ethereum block and its base fee.

mcps.name: Alchemy
mcps.summary: On-chain data suite (97 tools) — token prices, NFT holdings, transfers and full transaction history across 100+ chains. Fast, read-only market and wallet data.
mcps.example: Fetch the current USD price of ETH and its 24h change.

mcps.name: Solana (Jupiter)
mcps.summary: Jupiter, Solana's DeFi hub: looks up token prices, market cap, liquidity and swap routes across every Solana DEX. Multichain reach beyond Ethereum.
mcps.example: Look up BONK and give me its price, market cap and liquidity.

mcps.name: Recompute Kit
mcps.summary: The house verifier. Re-derives a claim from the primary artifact, pinned to an exact reference, with evidence attached. Don't trust - recompute.
mcps.example: Explain step by step how you would verify an on-chain attestation by recomputing it from the primary artifact.

mcps.name: Forensics
mcps.summary: Traces fund flows in and out of flagged addresses across chains and serves a scam-victim recovery playbook, following the money to downstream wallets.
mcps.example: Fetch the scam-victim recovery playbook and summarise the first steps a victim should take.

mcps.name: Uniswap
mcps.summary: Direct Uniswap v3: QuoterV2 price quotes (best fee tier auto-selected) + SwapRouter02 exactInputSingle calldata the user's own wallet signs. Ethereum + Base. Not an aggregator. Non-custodial and recomputable.
mcps.example: Swap 0.1 ETH into USDC on Uniswap (calldata for my wallet to sign).

mcps.name: 0G
mcps.summary: Writes/reads recompute artifacts (raw input / output / manifest blobs) on 0G Storage via @0gfoundation/0g-storage-ts-sdk, so agent actions are recomputable from decentralized data, not a single server. og_store_artifact returns a content-addressed rootHash; og_fetch_artifact retrieves it.
mcps.example: Store this action's recompute artifact on 0G and return its root hash.

mcps.name: ENS
mcps.summary: The first ENS write MCP: ens_check (availability + price), ens_register_commit / ens_register (commit-reveal purchase of a .eth name), ens_set_text / ens_set_addr / ens_set_primary, and ens_set_contenthash (point a name at an IPFS site — the name's real resolver is looked up on-chain, ENSIP-7 encoded). Non-custodial calldata the user's own wallet signs; supports ENSIP-25 agent-registration records and works on subnames. Existing ENS MCPs are read-only.
mcps.example: Is recompute.eth available? If so, register it, set its url record, and point its contenthash at ipfs://<cid> so it serves via eth.limo.
